text: The median time permitted for return to running after ACL reconstruction is 12 postoperative weeks, with a range of 5-39 weeks.

Fewer than one in five studies use clinical, strength, or performance-based criteria for return to running decisions after ACL reconstruction, despite best evidence recommending performance-based criteria combined with time-based criteria.
Return to running after ACL reconstruction should require full knee range of motion or greater than 95% of the non-injured knee.
Pain should be absent or less than 2 on a visual analogue scale before permitting return to running after ACL reconstruction.
Isometric extensor limb symmetry index should exceed 70%, and both extensor and flexor limb symmetry index should exceed 70%, before return to running after ACL reconstruction.
specific_value: extensor LSI >70%, extensor and flexor LSI >70%

text: Hop test limb symmetry index should exceed 70% before permitting return to running after ACL reconstruction.
Best evidence recommends combining performance-based criteria with time-based criteria rather than using time alone to determine readiness to return to running after ACL reconstruction.
